summary: Implemented the minimum M3 postwar governance boundary: explicit protocol command/query support, sim-core application path, auditable preview/projection read models, and deterministic 24-month scenario/property coverage for direct control, restored vassal ruler, and tribute-only settlements.

tests_run.result: Initial red test pass before implementation: 5 failed because postwar command/query support was absent.
Workspace typecheck passed for all packages with typecheck scripts.
Only task-scoped files were modified or added.
Node/worker hashes matched: hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd.
ESLint completed with no reported errors.
Prettier check passed for all matched files.
Architecture boundaries passed; determinism passed; root Vitest 24 files/151 tests passed; web Vitest 1 file/3 tests passed.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M3-POSTWAR-GOVERNANCE-001
tests_run.result: Task JSON parsed and shape check confirmed status REVIEW, route_to systems_architect, risk_level R3.

decisions: R3 protocol change: added GameCommand kind sim.apply-m3-postwar-governance plus query kinds sim.preview-m3-postwar-governance and sim.compare-m3-postwar-governance-outcomes for explicit command/query paths.
No save-format or permanent M3 save migration was introduced; existing requestSaveV1 still rejects WorldState state.m3 before emitting incompatible bytes.

The 24-month comparison is an auditable sim-core read-model projection derived from existing M2 stocks, M3 administrative burden, vassalage, obligations, and enfeoffment scaffolds; it does not run an M4 war loop or mutate resources.

Direct control changes the sole district controller to the victor and records direct administrative/garrison load; restored vassal and tribute-only settlements leave the local polity as sole controller while establishing the suzerain edge and only the method-specific obligations.
Duplicate postwar settlement obligation source prefixes are rejected with duplicate-obligation-settlement rather than stacking overlapping tribute/troop obligations.
risks: R3 public protocol surface changed and should receive systems_architect review for naming, long-term compatibility, and whether this remains acceptable inside M3 without save migration.
Projection coefficients are prototype constants for M3 validation, not final balance; balance_analyst can tune after architect acceptance.
The prototype intentionally does not model full diplomacy, legitimacy, rebellion, treaty text, or M4 campaign/battle/siege resolution.
requested_action: Review the R3 protocol/sim-core boundary, authority/ownership invariants, deterministic 24-month postwar outcomes, and confirm whether to accept or request changes.
